revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:飯高神社]]
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | name = Iitaka Shrine | location = {{ill|Sōsa|en|Sōsa|qid=Q858367|lt=Sōsa, Chiba|1=Sōsa}}, Iitaka 475, Chiba Prefecture | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |image = File:飯高神社.jpg | LatitudeDeg = 35 | LatitudeMin = 44 | LatitudeSec = 19 | LongitudeDeg = 140 | LongitudeMin = 31 | LongitudeSec = 49 | deity = {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|qid=Q2842779|1=Ame-no-Minakanushi}} | SacredObject = | ShrineRank = Former {{ill|Village Shrine|ja|村社|1=Son-sha|qid=Q134917284}}{{efn|Former shrine rank|lt=Son-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Village Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}} | Founding = Tenkei 1 (938) | MainHall = Copper-roofed shinmei-zukuri, 9 tsubo | WorshipHall = Tiled hip-and-gable roof, 20 tsubo | AnnualFestival = January 8 (Oshasha) | Ceremonies = | Map = Japan Chiba#Japan Kanto | location = {{ill|Sosa|qid=Q858367|1=Sōsa}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.73861111111111|N|140.53027777777777|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} }} '''Iitaka Shrine''' is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Sōsa|en|Sōsa|qid=Q858367|lt=Sōsa, Chiba|1=Sōsa}}, Iitaka, dedicated to the deity {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}. The shrine features a copper-roofed {{ill|Shinmei-zukuri|en|Shinmei-zukuri|qid=Q930545|lt=shinmei-zukuri|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} main hall (9 tsubo) and a worship hall with a tiled hip-and-gable roof (20 tsubo). The precincts measure 649 tsubo<ref name=":0">[From "Chiba Prefecture Shrine Register"]</ref>. The ceiling of the worship hall is adorned with 133 paintings of flowers, and the surrounding {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} features carvings of the {{ill|The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|en|The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|qid=Q1851166|lt=Twenty-Four Filial Exemplars|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars}}.
== History == According to shrine tradition, “During the Tenkei era, it was one of the six {{ill|Myōken|ja|妙見|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōken|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 妙見菩薩|1=Myōken|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q10960623}} sites worshipped by {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|en|Taira no Yoshifumi|qid=Q11483528|lt=Taira no Yoshifumi|1=Taira no Yoshifumi}}. Later, Yoshifumi’s descendant {{ill|Chiba Tsuneshige|en|Chiba Tsuneshige|qid=Q7676359|lt=Chiba Tsuneshige|1=Chiba Tsuneshige}} rebuilt it. Another account attributes its founding to Shinoda Nyuudou Keikū, a progenitor of the Hirayama family, who, after retreating to the Myōken Shrine in Chiba, was inspired by a miraculous vision of a sacred turtle and white snake at this location, leading to the construction of {{ill|Myōfuku-ji|ja|妙福寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Myōfuku-ji|1=myoufukuji|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11446980}}.”<ref name=":1">[From "History of Katori County"]</ref>
Taira no Yoshifumi, uncle of {{ill|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|ja|Taira no Masakado|1=Taira no Masakado|2=en|3=Taira no Masakado|4=ja|5=平将門|6=de|7=Taira no Masakado|8=es|9=Taira no Masakado|10=fr|11=Taira no Masakado|lt=Taira no Masakado|qid=Q1207652|1=Taira no Masakado|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|13=User:Immanuelle/Taira no Masakado|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, is remembered in conflicting legends—either as an ally or adversary of Masakado during the {{ill|Tengyō no Ran|en|Tengyō no Ran|qid=Q2975322|lt=Jōhei-Tengyō War|1=Tengyō no Ran}}.<ref name=":2">"An entry from the diary 'Gaiki' quoted in the 'Biography of Jōzō' mentions that someone, possibly Yoshifumi, relayed news of Masakado’s death to Abe no Tadayoshi immediately after the event, suggesting his allegiance to the opposing faction." [From "Chiba Prefectural History: Ancient Volume 2"]</ref> Legend holds that Yoshifumi, facing imminent defeat, was guided to the site by the voice of {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken Bosatsu|1=Myōken}}, leading to his subsequent success and the shrine’s continued association with the {{ill|Chiba clan|en|Chiba clan|qid=Q2975237|lt=Chiba clan|1=Chiba clan}} and {{ill|Iitaka clan|ja|飯高氏|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitaka clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.
The hill where the shrine now stands, known as Sakariyama, likely served as a fort during the Sengoku period and is associated with {{ill|Iitaka Castle|ja|飯高城|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitaka Castle|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Iitaka Temple|ja|飯高寺|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iitaka Temple|1=Hankō-ji Temple|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11666751}}.<ref name=":3">[From "Fortresses and Manor Sites of Tōsō"]</ref>
During the {{ill|Meiji Restoration|en|Meiji Restoration|qid=Q8707|lt=Meiji Restoration|1=Meiji Restoration}}, the Myōken image was transferred to Myōken-ji, and the shrine was rededicated to {{ill|Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja|天御中主命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ame-no-Minakanushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 天之御中主神}}, adopting the name Iitaka Shrine and becoming a village shrine.<ref name=":1">[From "History of Katori County"]</ref>
The shrine is a prime example of late 18th-century Shinto shrine carvings in the Shimōsa region. Its main hall is designated a tangible cultural property of Chiba Prefecture, while the painted ceiling of the worship hall and the {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=tamagaki|1=Sacred fences}} featuring the {{ill|The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|en|The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars|qid=Q1851166|lt=Twenty-Four Filial Exemplars|1=The Twenty-four Filial Exemplars}} carvings are designated cultural properties of Sōsa City. Similar tamagaki carvings can be found at {{ill|Narita-san|en|Narita-san|qid=Q3083875|lt=Naritasan Shinshoji|1=Narita-san}} and {{ill|Itsuna Shrine|ja|飯綱神社 (八千代市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Itsuna Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}.<ref name=":3">[From "Fortresses and Manor Sites of Tōsō"]</ref><ref name=":5">[From "New Edition: Iitaka Village Local History"]</ref>
[[File:飯高神社玉垣.jpg|thumb|Tamagaki carvings at Iitaka Shrine]] [[File:飯高神社拝殿天井絵.jpg|thumb|Ceiling paintings in the worship hall]]
== Timeline == * Tenkei 1 (938): consecration_year by Taira no Yoshifumi * Enkyō 3 (1310): Myōfuku-ji and Myōken Hall constructed by Shinoda Nyuudou Keikū * Kan’ei 4 (1627): Myōken Hall moved to Sakariyama following disputes between Myōfuku-ji and Iitaka Temple * Enkyō 4 (1747): Renovation * Taishō 4 (1915): Repairs to the main hall<ref name=":4">[From "Local History of Iitaka District"]</ref><ref name=":5">[From "New Edition: Iitaka Village Local History"]</ref>
== Current Status == The worship hall is severely damaged, supported by braces to prevent collapse. Reconstruction is hindered by the decline in the {{ill|Ujiko|ja|氏子|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ujiko|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 氏神|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Ujigami|qid=Q767655}} population, despite 50% financial support from the government.
== References == {{Reflist}}
== Bibliography == * "Chiba Prefecture Shrine Register," Chiba Prefecture Shrine Register Publishing Committee * "History of Katori County," Katori County, Chiba Prefecture * "Chiba Prefectural History: Ancient Volume 2," Chiba Prefecture Historical Materials Research Foundation * "Fortresses and Manor Sites of Tōsō," Shūeisha, Ryōshō Takamori and Kōichi Shiina * "Local History of Iitaka District," Iitaka Historical Preservation Society * "New Edition: Iitaka Village Local History," Yōkaichiba City History Compilation Committee
== Access == * '''By Road (Iitaka Danrin South Parking)''' ** {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Route 16|ja|千葉県道16号佐原八日市場線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Route 16|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 16|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406473}}: 30 minutes from Sawara; 10 minutes from Sōsa ** {{ill|Chiba Prefectural Route 74|ja|千葉県道74号多古笹本線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Chiba Prefectural Route 74|1=Chiba Prefectural Road Route 74|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11406657}}: 1 minute from Iitaka Intersection toward Sōsa ** {{ill|Tōsō Kōiki Agricultural Road|ja|東総広域農道|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōsō Kōiki Agricultural Road|1=Tōsō Kōiki Agricultural Road|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11528184}}: 1 minute from Route 16 intersection toward Sawara * '''By Train''' ** Nearest station: {{ill|Yōkaichiba Station|en|Yōkaichiba Station|qid=Q5363766|lt=Yōkaichiba Station|1=Yōkaichiba Station}}, JR East Sōbu Main Line
{{Shinto-stub}} {{Pref-stub|pref=Chiba Prefecture}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See Also == * {{ill|Taira no Yoshifumi|en|Taira no Yoshifumi|qid=Q11483528|lt=Taira no Yoshifumi|1=Taira no Yoshifumi}} * {{ill|Myōken|en|Myōken|qid=Q10960623|lt=Myōken|1=Myōken}} * {{ill|Shimōsa Province|en|Shimōsa Province|qid=Q1053695|lt=Shimōsa Province|1=Shimōsa Province}}
